pandoc [OPTIONS] [FILES]
Input formats:  commonmark, docx, epub, html, json, latex, markdown, mediawiki,
                odt, org, rst, textile
Output formats: asciidoc, beamer, commonmark, docx, epub, html, html5, json,
                latex, man, markdown, mediawiki, odt, org, pdf, plain, pptx,
                revealjs, rst, rtf, textile
Options:
  -f FORMAT, -r FORMAT, --from=FORMAT, --read=FORMAT
                           input format
  -t FORMAT, -w FORMAT, --to=FORMAT, --write=FORMAT
                           output format
  -o FILE, --output=FILE   output file (use - for stdout)
      --data-dir=DIRECTORY user data directory
  -d FILE, --defaults=FILE specify a set of default option settings
  -M KEY[:VALUE], --metadata=KEY[:VALUE]
                           set document metadata field
      --standalone, -s     produce output with an appropriate header and footer
      --template=FILE      use the specified file as a custom template
  -V KEY[:VALUE], --variable=KEY[:VALUE]
                           set a template variable
      --toc, --table-of-contents
                           include an automatically generated table of contents
      --toc-depth=NUMBER   number of section levels to include in the TOC
  -N, --number-sections    number section headings
      --highlight-style=STYLE|FILE
                           specify coloring style for highlighted source code
      --pdf-engine=PROGRAM use the specified engine when producing PDF output
                           (pdflatex, xelatex, lualatex, wkhtmltopdf, weasyprint)
      --resource-path=SEARCHPATH
                           search path for images and other resources
  -c URL, --css=URL        link to a CSS style sheet (may be used repeatedly)
      --self-contained     produce a standalone HTML file with no external deps
      --embed-resources    produce a standalone file with all resources embedded
  -H FILE, --include-in-header=FILE
                           include contents of FILE at the end of the header
      --mathml             convert TeX math to MathML
      --mathjax[=URL]      use MathJax to display embedded TeX math in HTML
      --wrap=auto|none|preserve
                           how to wrap text in the output
      --columns=NUMBER     length of lines in characters
      --fail-if-warnings   exit with error status if there are any warnings
      --verbose            give verbose debugging output
      --quiet              suppress warning messages
      --version            print version
  -h, --help               show this help message
